definitions.definition: Amines are organic compounds that contain a functional group derived from ammonia (NH3) in which one or more hydrogen atoms are replaced by alkyl or aryl groups.
definitions.example: Examples of amines include methylamine (CH3NH2), ethylamine (C2H5NH2), and aniline (C6H5NH2).

definitions.definition: Aliphatic amines are amines that do not contain a benzene ring in their molecule.
definitions.example: Examples of aliphatic amines include methylamine (CH3NH2), ethylamine (C2H5NH2), and propylamine (C3H7NH2).

definitions.definition: Aromatic amines are amines that contain a benzene ring in their molecule.
definitions.example: Examples of aromatic amines include aniline (C6H5NH2), N-methylaniline (C6H5N(CH3)H), and N,N-dimethylaniline (C6H3N(CH3)2).

definitions.definition: Primary amines are amines in which the amino group (-NH2) is attached to a primary carbon atom.
definitions.example: Examples of primary amines include methylamine (CH3NH2), ethylamine (C2H5NH2), and propylamine (C3H7NH2).

definitions.definition: Secondary amines are amines in which the amino group (-NH-) is attached to two carbon atoms.
definitions.example: Examples of secondary amines include dimethylamine ((CH3)2NH), diethylamine ((C2H5)2NH), and dibutylamine ((C4H9)2NH).

definitions.definition: Tertiary amines are amines in which the amino group (-N-) is attached to three carbon atoms.
definitions.example: Examples of tertiary amines include trimethylamine ((CH3)3N), triethylamine ((C2H5)3N), and tributylamine ((C4H9)3N).

definitions.definition: Monobasic amines are amines that have only one amino group.
definitions.example: Examples of monobasic amines include methylamine (CH3NH2), ethylamine (C2H5NH2), and propylamine (C3H7NH2).

definitions.definition: Dibasic amines are amines that have two amino groups.
definitions.example: Examples of dibasic amines include ethylenediamine (H2NCH2CH2NH2).

definitions.definition: Basicity of amines is a measure of their ability to accept a proton.
definitions.example: Primary amines are more basic than secondary amines, which are more basic than tertiary amines.

definitions.definition: pKb value is a measure of the basic strength of an amine.
definitions.example: Amines with lower pKb values are more basic than those with higher pKb values.

definitions.definition: Acidic character of amines refers to their ability to donate a proton.
definitions.example: Amines with strong electron-withdrawing groups attached to the amino group exhibit acidic character.

definitions.definition: Electron-donating groups are groups that release electrons and increase the basicity of amines.
definitions.example: Alkyl groups and aryl groups are electron-donating groups.

definitions.definition: Electron-withdrawing groups are groups that withdraw electrons and decrease the basicity of amines.
definitions.example: Halogen atoms and nitro groups are electron-withdrawing groups.

definitions.definition: Substitution reaction of amines involves the replacement of a hydrogen atom of the amino group with a substituent.
definitions.example: Examples of substitution reactions of amines include alkylation and acylation of amines.

definitions.definition: Elimination reaction of amines involves the removal of a hydrogen atom and a substituent from the amino group.
definitions.example: Examples of elimination reactions of amines include deamination reactions.
